highlights: Build 3: corpus discovered live as rag_corpus (21,349 chunks) — adopted, not ingested; live relevance verified through the production embedder seam the same session
Ch 7 boundary enforced three ways (deps, imports, raw-SQL scan) after review found the raw-sql bypass
Build 4: §6.15 shipped exactly as specced, unit-tested against the handbook's own worked examples (3 revealed obs → 0.65)
trait_observations doubles as evidence ledger and tuning instrumentation; every policy decision recorded incl. veto applications
learnings: Adversarial review before tag caught two P1-class issues (raw-SQL boundary bypass; TETHR_DATABASE_URL live-wipe footgun) — the gate earns its cost every single time, even run in-context when subagent quota is exhausted
Embeddings must never cross-provider fallback: a different model is a different vector space; guard dimension AND model identity at the seam
Shared-cluster integration suites need own-database isolation plus file serialization; cluster-global objects (roles) still couple them — drop scratch DBs in afterAll
pgvector test vectors must be constructed non-orthogonal when asserting order; cosine ties are nondeterministic under HNSW
Env-var naming is a safety surface: the live DSN gets a name the test runner can never mistake for disposable
debt: Live Supabase carries a prototype schema that predates our migration chain — baseline decision needed before first deploy
deploy-staging red (Vercel secrets, CEO-owned) since Build 0
Write-path extract/abstract model wiring deferred to Build 6 (first real episodes)
next: Build 5 — Messaging Substrate & Interaction Shell (Photon Spectrum + Next.js shell). See docs/handoffs/build-5-handoff.md.
